breach.summary: Major credit reporting agency experienced large-scale breach exposing Social Security numbers, dates of birth, and credit card information.
Hotel chain breach exposed guest payment cards and personal information across multiple properties via compromised reservation system.
Financial services company experienced breach affecting customers and credit card applicants due to misconfigured firewall.
Major retailer's point-of-sale systems compromised during holiday shopping season, exposing millions of customer payment cards.
Title insurance company exposed sensitive financial documents including bank account numbers and social security numbers.
Health insurance provider experienced breach affecting members nationwide, exposing personal and medical information.
Email platform breach compromised nearly 3 billion user accounts, one of the largest data breaches in history.
Retailer's point-of-sale systems exposed customer payment card information through malware installed at checkout terminals.
Insurance company experienced breach exposing employee and customer personal information through email compromise.
Social media platform exposed user phone numbers and personal data through misconfigured API endpoints.
Healthcare provider left unencrypted patient records in publicly accessible S3 bucket for 3 weeks, exposing names, SSNs, and medical histories.
Threat actor gained access to Okta's support system, potentially exposing customer metadata and session tokens used for multifactor authentication.
Remote Code Execution vulnerability in Exchange Server allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code and access mailboxes.
Exposed driver personal information including names, phone numbers, email addresses, and vehicle details on misconfigured database.
Tax information and payment details for Twitch streamers leaked through compromised database, affecting creators worldwide.
Threat actors used stolen API keys to access and retrieve signed documents containing personal and financial information.
Misconfigured sharing settings exposed internal company wikis, roadmaps, and credentials in multiple Notion workspaces.
Multiple e-commerce stores on Shopify compromised via weak admin passwords, leading to customer payment card theft.
Developers left MongoDB instances publicly accessible without authentication, exposing customer databases with PII.
Legacy Slack bot tokens exposed in GitHub repositories, allowing attackers to access private channels and user data.

The D1R cybercrime group claimed to have stolen valuable data from Synopsys and Bosch, threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. The post Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.
breach.org: Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims

breach.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps...
breach.org: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak

cve.summary: Remote code execution vulnerability in Cisco IOS XE allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain EXEC access.
Authentication bypass in Confluence allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.
SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer affecting millions of users worldwide.
Heap-based buffer overflow in FortiOS allowing remote code execution on firewalls.
Authentication bypass in SolarWinds Orion Platform allowing unauthorized access to monitoring systems.
Remote code execution in vCenter Server affecting virtualized infrastructure globally.
Command injection in PAN-OS allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands on firewalls.
Remote code execution in Microsoft Exchange Server allowing unauthenticated attackers to compromise email systems.
OGNL injection vulnerability in Apache Struts allowing remote code execution in web applications.
Privilege escalation vulnerability in IBM Db2 allowing local users to gain administrative access.
Remote Code Execution in Log4j 2.x via JNDI injection in logging messages. CVSS 10.0.
Use-after-free vulnerability in V8 JavaScript engine allowing arbitrary code execution. Exploit available.
Privilege escalation in Windows Kernel allowing local users to gain SYSTEM access. Active exploitation detected.
Buffer overflow in OpenSSL certificate validation allowing DoS and potential RCE. Affects versions 1.1.1 and 3.0.x.
Type confusion vulnerability in PHP's filter functions allowing information disclosure and RCE.
Prototype pollution vulnerability in jQuery allowing XSS attacks in web applications.
Race condition in Linux kernel memory management allowing privilege escalation on multi-core systems.
HTTP request smuggling vulnerability in nginx HTTP/2 module allowing request injection attacks.
New CPU microarchitecture side-channel attack (Spectre variant) affecting Intel processors from 2010-2026.
Information disclosure in AWS Lambda environment variables leaking database credentials and API keys.

threat.summary: REvil ransomware gang conducting high-profile attacks demanding millions in ransom from Fortune 500 companies.
DarkSide responsible for Colonial Pipeline ransomware attack, causing nationwide fuel supply disruption.
Conti ransomware crew conducting double-extortion attacks against healthcare and critical infrastructure.
Russian APT29 conducting sophisticated espionage campaign against government and enterprise networks.
Chinese APT41 conducting cyber espionage and financially motivated attacks against global enterprises.
FIN7 cybercriminal group targeting financial institutions and hospitality sector with sophisticated malware.
North Korean Lazarus Group conducting cryptocurrency theft and targeting critical infrastructure globally.
North Korean Kimsuky conducting spear phishing campaigns against government and research institutions.
Advanced persistent threat group Equation Group maintaining sophisticated cyber espionage infrastructure.
Russian cybercriminal group Silence targeting banking systems and financial institutions for theft.
Scattered Spider APT conducting supply chain attacks targeting managed service providers, stealing credentials to access customer environments.
LockBit ransomware gang claims responsibility for 45 attacks in May alone, targeting healthcare and critical infrastructure.
ALPHV continues targeting Fortune 500 companies with double-extortion attacks, demanding record ransom payments.
FIN7 linked to stealing $100M+ in cryptocurrency through compromised exchange accounts and DeFi protocols.
Wizard Spider targeting US healthcare networks with TrickBot and Conti ransomware, causing significant operational disruptions.
Evil Corp maintaining infrastructure for ransomware and botnet operations despite sanctioning, targeting financial services.
Russian state-sponsored APT28 conducting espionage against NATO members and critical defense contractors.
Russian APT Turla conducting long-term espionage operations against government and diplomatic targets worldwide.
Emotet botnet returning with new evasion techniques after disruption in 2021, spreading via phishing and USB drives.
Chinese military-linked APT groups conducting massive campaign against US critical infrastructure, stealing operational technology data.

Four compromised npm packages in the @asyncapi namespace have been observed distributing a multi-stage botnet loader, according to findings from OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity. The affected packages are listed below - @asyncapi/generator-helpers@1.1.1 @asyncapi/generator-components@0.7.1...
threat.actor: Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware

threat.summary: At least two distinct threat actors are weaponizing a novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing in cloud campaigns, while slipping past telemetry. The activity allows users to enumerate user accounts and validate stolen credentials in Microsoft Entra ID environments, without ever generating a...
threat.actor: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials

threat.summary: LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]
threat.actor: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts

threat.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments. "LabubaRAT creates a reusable foothold for hands-on activity," Blackpoint Cyber researchers Sam Decker and Nevan Beal said...
threat.actor: LabubaRAT Masquerades as NVIDIA Software to Control Windows Hosts

threat.summary: Bitdefender researchers show how Windows bind links can create conflicting filesystem views to hide malware from endpoint security products. The post Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools appeared first on SecurityWeek.
threat.actor: Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools

threat.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after...
threat.actor: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet

threat.summary: A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from JFrog. The packages did not go after the developers who might install them. The operators used the registry as free...
threat.actor: 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet

news.title: Biden Signs Executive Order on Cybersecurity
news.summary: President Biden signed comprehensive cybersecurity executive order strengthening critical infrastructure protection.

news.title: NIST Publishes Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
news.summary: National Institute of Standards and Technology released updated cybersecurity framework with new controls.

news.title: Cybersecurity Regulation Tightens Globally
news.summary: European Union and other nations implement stricter cybersecurity regulations for critical infrastructure operators.

news.title: Supply Chain Attacks Double in 2025
news.summary: Report shows supply chain attacks increased 200% in 2025, with SolarWinds-style incidents becoming more common.

news.title: Cloud Security Spending Surges
news.summary: Cloud security market experiencing 30% annual growth as enterprises invest heavily in protection for cloud environments.

news.title: Incident Response as a Service Grows
news.summary: Managed incident response services seeing increased adoption as organizations struggle with cyber incident response.

news.title: Ransomware Demands Hit $30M Average
news.summary: Report indicates average ransomware ransom demand increased to $30 million, targeting only highest-value targets.

news.title: MFA Fatigue Attacks on Rise
news.summary: Attackers increasingly exploiting user fatigue with multi-factor authentication prompts to gain account access.

news.title: Identity and Access Management Critical
news.summary: Security experts emphasize IAM as critical control point for defending against modern cyber threats.

news.title: Zero Trust Adoption Accelerates
news.summary: More organizations moving to zero-trust security models after high-profile breaches demonstrate perimeter defense failures.

news.title: CISA Releases New Security Guidance
news.summary: CISA published updated secure software development framework addressing supply chain security and zero-trust architecture.

news.title: EU Digital Services Act Enforcement Begins
news.summary: European Union begins enforcement of Digital Services Act with first round of compliance checks on major tech platforms.

news.title: Quantum Computing Threatens Encryption
news.summary: New quantum computer achieves cryptographically relevant breakthrough, prompting urgent migration to post-quantum cryptography.

news.title: AI-Powered Phishing Attacks Surge
news.summary: Cybersecurity firms report 400% increase in sophisticated phishing emails generated by large language models.

news.title: Zero-Trust Architecture Adoption Growing
news.summary: Gartner reports 60% of enterprises now implementing zero-trust network architecture as default security model.

news.title: Ransomware Payments Hit Record High
news.summary: Chainalysis reports ransomware payments exceeded $2.5 billion in 2025, with healthcare sector accounting for 32% of payments.

news.title: Password-Less Authentication Adoption
news.summary: Microsoft reports 50% of new Azure AD logins now use passwordless authentication methods like Windows Hello.

news.title: IoT Botnet Reaches 15M Devices
news.summary: New Mirai variant infecting 15 million IoT devices worldwide, used for DDoS and cryptocurrency mining operations.

news.title: Cybersecurity Skills Shortage Worsens
news.summary: Industry report shows 3.5M cybersecurity job openings unfilled globally, leading to burnout among existing security professionals.

news.title: Bug Bounty Programs Break Records
news.summary: HackerOne and Bugcrowd combined disbursed over $500M in bug bounties, with white-hat hackers finding critical flaws at scale.

kev.action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.

patch.summary: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update, which includes the July 2026 Patch Tuesday security updates for 570 vulnerabilities, along with additional security fixes. [...]
patch.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update

patch.summary: Today is Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday, and with it comes security updates for a record-breaking 570 flaws, including two zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in attacks and one publicly disclosed. [...]
patch.title: Microsoft July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes massive 570 flaws, 3 zero-days

patch.summary: Microsoft has released Windows 11's June 2026 Update for version 25H2, 24H2, and 23H2, bringing fixes for over 570 security issues. [...]
patch.title: Windows 11 KB5101650 & KB5099414 cumulative updates released

patch.summary: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates. [...]
patch.title: SonicWall warns of SMA1000 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks, patch now

patch.summary: Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. The release covers 622 of Microsoft's own CVEs by its Security Update Guide count, more than triple June's previous high of around 200. Those two live bugs are the ones to grab first....
patch.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack

patch.summary: SAP has rolled out updates to address multiple vulnerabilities as part of its July 2026 security updates, including a critical flaw in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-44747 (CVSS score: 9.9), an out-of-bounds write flaw that allows an authenticated attacker to...
patch.title: SAP Patches CVSS 9.9 NetWeaver ABAP Flaw That Could Expose or Modify Data

patch.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to...
patch.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws

patch.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at...
patch.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026

patch.summary: Microsoft is blocking this month's Windows 11 security updates on some Dell devices because they are causing shutdowns and performance issues. [...]
patch.title: Microsoft: Some Dell PCs shut down after recent Windows updates

patch.summary: Three of the 622 CVEs for which Microsoft issued patches this week are zero-days; there are more than 60 critical vulnerabilities.
patch.title: Records Are Made to Be Broken: Patch Tuesday Raises Triage Stakes

patch.summary: The industrial giants fixed dozens of vulnerabilities across their ICS products, with advisories also released by CISA and VDE CERT. The post ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Rockwell appeared first on SecurityWeek.
patch.title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Rockwell

ransomware.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real...
ransomware.group: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’

ransomware.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious activities, including ransomware attacks against Americans. The VPN, named First VPN Service (1VPNS), has been accused...
ransomware.group: U.S. Sanctions First VPN Service and Malware Cryptor Seller Over Ransomware Support

ransomware.summary: Microsoft signed a malicious kernel driver, and now it's being used to kill security software in ransomware attacks.
ransomware.group: 'GodDamn' Ransomware Uses BYOVD to Smite US Companies

ransomware.summary: U.S. federal prosecutors have unsealed charges against three Russian nationals, accusing them of providing bulletproof hosting (BPH) services to ransomware gangs that caused over $62 million in damages to victims worldwide. [...]
ransomware.group: US charges alleged operators of Russian bulletproof hosting service

ransomware.summary: Email attacks overtook exploits as the top ransomware root cause last year. Multifactor authentication (MFA) was deployed in 97% of credential-based attacks but failed to prevent compromise.
ransomware.group: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
